parts.content:
Write 1 dissimilar SBA question(s) on: 'Obesity supine — reduced functional residual capacity'.

Each question should test application of knowledge, not simple recall — a candidate should need to reason from principles rather than just retrieve a fact. Use the retrieve tool to find relevant reference material before writing. Return the result as a QuestionSet.

parts.args: {"query": 'In obesity, particularly when supine and anaesthetised, functional residual capacity falls and may approach or fall below closing capacity.'}

{"topic": "Primary Frca Sample Sbas", "exam": "primary", "curriculum_node_code": null, "model": "openai:gpt-5.5", "generated_at": "2026-05-02T01:16:45.156172", "questions": [{"stem": "A patient with a body mass index of 50 kg m-2 and no other notable medical history is positioned from semi-sitting to horizontal for induction of general anaesthesia.", "lead": "Which respiratory change is most important when this patient lies flat?", "options": [{"text": "Chest compliance will decrease", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Chest wall compliance does decrease in obesity and supine positioning, but this is less immediately important than the fall in functional residual capacity."}, {"text": "Functional residual capacity will decrease", "is_correct": true, "explanation": "Supine positioning in morbid obesity markedly reduces functional residual capacity, promoting airway closure, shunt, hypoxaemia, and rapid desaturation during apnoea."}, {"text": "Inspiratory reserve volume will increase", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Inspiratory reserve volume may change with positioning, but this is not the key clinically important effect compared with reduced FRC."}, {"text": "Peak expiratory flow rate will decrease", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Peak expiratory flow may decrease, but it is not the dominant concern during induction."}, {"text": "Respiratory rate will increase", "is_correct": false, "explanation": "Respiratory rate may be higher in obesity, but the positional change's most important effect is reduced FRC."}], "explanation": "In obesity, particularly when supine and anaesthetised, functional residual capacity falls and may approach or fall below closing capacity. This increases atelectasis and shunt and reduces the oxygen reservoir, causing rapid desaturation during apnoea.", "curriculum_node_codes": ["1_GA_B_12", "1_GA_B_25", "1_GA_B_13", "1_GA_H_4", "1_GA_I_1"], "curriculum_node_scores": [0.6480269089984176, 0.6196490613725061, 0.6028511021056868, 0.5878945321552393, 0.5681566534763913], "title": "Obesity supine — reduced functional residual capacity"}]}
